posts.title: Choosing the Right Business Card Stock
posts.excerpt: Matte, glossy, soft-touch, or textured? A plain-English guide to picking the paper that matches your brand.
posts.body_md:
Picking a paper **stock** is the single biggest decision for how your business card *feels* in someone's hand. Here's how to choose.

## Weight matters first

Stock weight is measured in gsm (grams per square meter). For premium cards we recommend **400gsm or higher** — anything lighter feels flimsy.

- 300gsm — standard, everyday cards
- 400gsm — premium, the sweet spot
- 600gsm — ultra-thick, luxury feel

## Finish changes everything

![Soft-touch matte business card](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1524592094714-0f0654e20314?w=1280&q=80)

A **soft-touch matte** laminate gives a velvety surface that resists fingerprints. Glossy finishes make colors pop but show smudges.

> Pro tip: pair a matte finish with spot-UV on your logo for a tactile contrast that people can't stop touching.

Ready to try a few? Browse our [business card products](/products/business-cards) and order a sample pack.

posts.title: 5 Sticker Design Mistakes to Avoid
posts.excerpt: Bleed, safe zones, and resolution — the small details that ruin a sticker print. Here's how to get them right.
posts.body_md:
Stickers look simple, but a few common mistakes lead to disappointing prints. Avoid these five.

## 1. Forgetting the bleed

Always extend your background **3mm past the cut line**. Without bleed, you'll get thin white slivers along the edges.

## 2. Text too close to the edge

Keep important text inside the **safe zone** — at least 3mm from the cut. Die-cutting has a small tolerance.

## 3. Low-resolution artwork

Upload artwork at **300 DPI** at final size. A logo that looks crisp on screen can pixelate when printed.

## 4. Ignoring the cut shape

Die-cut, kiss-cut, and circle cuts each frame your design differently. Design with the final shape in mind.

## 5. Skipping a proof

Always review the digital proof. It's the cheapest insurance against a costly reprint.

Want the full spec sheet? Check our [sticker products](/products/stickers).
